statement: Temporal remains the best default for judge-gym only when judged against explicit rejection criteria for Restate and Inngest, centered on first-class interactive control, strong server-side execution inspection, and long-lived workflow semantics.
rationale: Temporal is still the most grounded fit, but the alternatives are credible enough that the choice must be defended with explicit requirement-level criteria rather than broad claims.
keyAssumptions: Judge-gym values interactive per-process control and execution introspection more than lighter-weight deployment ergonomics
The team accepts Node-worker and self-hosted orchestration constraints
validationCriteria: The rewrite documents why Restate and Inngest are rejected or deferred against concrete requirements
Temporal's advantages are stated in terms of judge-gym needs rather than general durable execution marketing
critiquePoints: The alternatives matrix remains the least grounded pass-3 area
questions: Which exact judge-gym requirements would cause Restate or Inngest to win instead?
